intro: APT37 (ScarCruft, InkySquid), ClickFix, and Pushpaganda were the most active threat actors in a concentrated wave of social-engineering attacks documented between April 8 and April 20, 2026. The 36 articles in this archive detail 25 high-severity incidents, alongside 10 medium and one informational report. These campaigns primarily targeted the financial-services, cryptocurrency, and hospitality sectors, with significant impact on individuals and logistics. Activity was observed globally, with notable focus on the United States, Canada, the United Kingdom, and Australia.
